title: notifications core — model relationships
description: The 6 notifications core entities + their relationships to each other + to foreign models (User in `user`, Workspace / Application in `workspaces`). Channel-specific side state (push subscriptions, mail suppressions, sms opt-outs) belongs to the respective channel module and does not appear here. CI verifies every named relation resolves to a defined model or an inbound target from another module.

internal.description: A notification fans out to N per-channel deliveries. Cascade delete on hard-purge.
Categorises the notification for preference resolution + analytics.
The resolved template at send time. Null on failed template-resolution (renders a fallback + fires NOTIFICATIONS_TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND).
Pivot table snapshotting which notifications composed a delivered digest. Retained for audit.

outbound.description: Addressee. Null when addressee_type = anonymous (guest invitations, invite-by-email before user creation).
The user who caused the notification to be sent. Null when system-triggered (scheduled digests, maintenance alerts).
Hard-cascade on user delete (GDPR Art. 17 erasure).
NULL = platform default template; workspace_id set = workspace override.

inbound.description: Downstream modules relate INTO notifications by category_slug (which the module publishes via notifications.json) OR by dispatching a BaseNotification subclass. No downstream module holds a foreign key to a Notification row — notifications is a fire-and-forget append-only log.
conventions: Modules that reference a specific delivery (rare; typically audit / support use-cases) do so via notification_id + notification_delivery_id as ULIDs — no cross-module FK constraints.
Channel-specific side state (push subscriptions, mail suppressions, sms opt-outs) belongs to the respective channel module.
Downstream consumers subscribe to core events (NotificationDelivered, NotificationFailed) + write their own state — never SELECT from notification_deliveries directly.

morphs.description: Anonymous-addressee case where the notification pre-dates a User row (invite-by-email). addressee_type/addressee_id polymorphic; NULL both when the email is truly external (rare).
The trigger. `system` sentinel for scheduled / automated notifications.
